name: Rita Hayworth
abstract: Rita Hayworth (born Margarita Carmen Cansino; October 17, 1918 – May 14, 1987) was an American actress, dancer, and pin-up girl. She achieved fame in the 1940s as one of the top stars of the Golden Age of Hollywood, and appeared in 61 films over 38 years.

about_quotes.speaker: Luther Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (Anita Ellis) is the voice of Rita Hayworth, but the text describes a professional recording/voice-over relationship rather than a personal one. The interviewee does not mention meeting or interacting with Rita Hayworth personally.
about_quotes.doc_title: Luther Henderson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Green
about_quotes.evidence: And Rita Hayworth, you see. ... which also happened to Eleanor Powell and Arlene Judge, Rita Hayworth, Lucille Ball, Joan Blondell -- she used to come there -- and Nancy Carroll, she was famous in the movies, Warner Brothers movie star.

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: we also did the "Jolson Story" there, and did a couple of things on Rita Hayworth
